words.def: the steady growth of something so that it becomes more advanced, stronger, etc.
words.example: a baby’s development in the womb

words.def: an object or a piece of equipment that has been designed to do a particular job
words.example: a tracking/recording/listening device

words.def: a simple drawing using lines to explain where something is, how something works, etc.
words.example: The results are shown in diagram 2.

words.def: a conversation in a book, play or film or in language teaching materials
words.example: Learners are asked to listen to three short dialogues.

words.def: a clear precious stone of pure carbon, the hardest substance known. Diamonds are used in jewellery and also in industry, especially for cutting glass.
words.example: a diamond ring/necklace

words.def: a problem; a thing or situation that causes problems
words.example: the difficulties of English syntax

words.def: using a system of receiving and sending information as a series of the numbers one and zero, showing that an electronic signal is there or is not there
words.example: a digital camera

words.def: going in the straightest line between two places without stopping or changing direction
words.example: the most direct route

words.def: without stopping or changing direction
words.example: The path leads directly to the river.

words.def: any substance that makes something dirty, for example dust, soil or mud
words.example: His clothes were covered in dirt.

words.def: something that causes problems and tends to stop somebody/something from succeeding or making progress
words.example: a serious/severe/significant disadvantage

words.def: upset because something you hoped for has not happened or been as good, successful, etc. as you expected
words.example: The singer has promised to refund any disappointed fans.

words.def: not as good, successful, etc. as you had hoped; making you feel disappointed
words.example: a disappointing result/performance/defeat

words.def: an unexpected event, such as a very bad accident, a flood or a fire, that kills a lot of people or causes a lot of damage
words.example: the world's worst humanitarian disaster

words.def: an act or the process of finding somebody/something, or learning about something that was not known about before
words.example: Researchers in this field have made some important new discoveries.

words.def: an illness affecting humans, animals or plants, often caused by infection
words.example: heart/lung/liver disease

words.def: a feeling of not liking somebody/something
words.example: dislike of somebody/something, He did not try to hide his dislike of his boss.

words.def: the amount of space between two places or things
words.example: a short/long distance

words.def: no longer married because your marriage has been legally ended
words.example: My parents are divorced.

words.def: a film or a radio or television programme giving facts about something
words.example: There were some interesting interviews in the documentary.

words.def: twice as much or as many as usual
words.example: a double helping

words.def: a feeling of being uncertain about something or not believing something
words.example: a feeling of doubt and uncertainty

words.def: on a floor of a house or building lower than the one you are on, especially the one at ground level
words.example: a downstairs bathroom

words.def: wearing clothes and not naked or wearing clothes for sleeping
words.example: Hurry up and get dressed.

words.def: a very small amount of liquid that forms a round shape
words.example: The first drops of rain fell.

words.def: a musical instrument made of a hollow round frame with plastic or skin stretched tightly across one or both ends. You play it by hitting it with sticks or with your hands.
words.example: to play the drum

words.def: having drunk so much alcohol that it is impossible to think or speak clearly
words.example: They were clearly too drunk to drive.

words.def: caused by somebody/something; because of somebody/something
words.example: The team's success was largely due to her efforts.

words.def: a fine powder that consists of very small pieces of sand, earth, etc.
words.example: A cloud of dust rose as the truck drove off.

words.def: something that you feel you have to do because it is your moral or legal responsibility
words.example: duty to do something, It is my duty to report it to the police.

words.def: a sudden, violent shaking of the earth’s surface
words.example: a devastating/massive/powerful earthquake

words.def: located in the east or facing east
words.example: eastern Spain

words.def: connected with the trade, industry and development of wealth of a country, an area or a society
words.example: They discussed social, economic and political issues.

words.def: the relationship between production, trade and the supply of money in a particular country or region
words.example: The economy is in recession.

words.def: the outside limit of an object, a surface or an area; the part furthest from the centre
words.example: I gripped the edge of my desk to steady myself.

words.def: a person who is in charge of a newspaper, magazine, etc., or part of one, and who decides what should be included
words.example: the editor of the Washington Post

words.def: to teach somebody over a period of time at a school, university, etc.
words.example: be educated, She was educated in the US.

words.def: connected with education
words.example: children with special educational needs

words.def: producing the result that is wanted or intended; producing a successful result
words.example: Aspirin is a simple but highly effective treatment.

words.def: an attempt to do something especially when it is difficult to do
words.example: The project was a team effort.

words.def: the process of choosing a person or a group of people for a position, especially a political position, by voting
words.example: presidential/parliamentary/local elections

words.def: connected with electricity; using or producing electricity
words.example: an electrical fault in the engine

words.def: a form of energy from charged elementary particles, usually supplied as electric current through cables, wires, etc. for lighting, heating, driving machines, etc.
words.example: materials that conduct electricity

words.def: having or using many small parts, such as microchips, that control and direct a small electric current
words.example: Airline passengers no longer have to turn off all electronic devices while on the plane.

words.def: a necessary or typical part of something; one of several parts that something contains
words.example: There are three important elements to consider.

words.def: shy, uncomfortable or ashamed, especially in a social situation
words.example: I've never felt so embarrassed in my life!

words.def: making you feel shy, uncomfortable or ashamed
words.example: an embarrassing moment/situation

words.def: a sudden serious and dangerous event or situation that needs immediate action to deal with it
words.example: This is a medical emergency needing urgent treatment with antibiotics.

words.def: a strong feeling such as love, fear or anger; the part of a person’s character that consists of feelings
words.example: to show/express your emotions

words.def: work, especially when it is done to earn money; the state of being employed
words.example: full-time/part-time employment

words.def: with no people or things inside
words.example: an empty box/glass/bottle

words.def: to give somebody support, courage or hope
words.example: encourage somebody, We were greatly encouraged by the positive response of the public.

words.def: a person who hates somebody or who acts or speaks against somebody/something
words.example: She didn't have an enemy in the world.

words.def: having agreed to marry somebody
words.example: When did you get engaged?

words.def: the part of a vehicle that produces power to make the vehicle move
words.example: a diesel/petrol engine

words.def: the activity of applying scientific knowledge to the design, building and control of machines, roads, bridges, electrical equipment, etc.
words.example: The bridge is a triumph of modern engineering.

words.def: to interest somebody or make somebody laugh in order to please them
words.example: The aim of the series is both to entertain and inform.

words.def: films, music, etc. used to entertain people; an example of this
words.example: radio, television and other forms of entertainment

words.def: a door, gate, passage, etc. used for entering a room, building or place
words.example: the front/back/side entrance of the house

words.def: an act of going into or getting into a place
words.example: She made her entry to the sound of thunderous applause.

words.def: connected with the natural conditions in which people, animals and plants live; connected with the environment
words.example: the environmental impact of pollution

words.def: one part of a story that is broadcast on television or radio in several parts
words.example: The next episode has not yet been filmed.

words.def: to the same degree; in the same or in a similar way
words.example: Diet and exercise are equally important.

words.def: the things that are needed for a particular purpose or activity
words.example: a useful piece of equipment for the kitchen

words.def: the act or a method of escaping from a place or an unpleasant or dangerous situation
words.example: I had a narrow escape (= I was lucky to have escaped).

words.def: completely necessary; extremely important in a particular situation or for a particular activity
words.example: an essential part/feature of something

words.def: at the end of a period of time or a series of events
words.example: Our flight eventually left five hours late.

words.def: correct in every detail
words.example: The new palace is an exact replica of the original building.

words.def: to consider or study an idea, a subject, etc. very carefully
words.example: examine something, This study sets out to examine in detail the possible effects of climate change.
